html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 钦定四库全书 - Formal title meaning 'Imperially Designated Four Treasuries of Books.' Refers to the imperial compilation of Chinese literature during the Qing dynasty.
  • 子部十二 - Refers to the twelfth category of the imperial compilation, specifically the 'Zi' section, which includes philosophical and literary works.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 钦定 - Indicates that the work was officially sanctioned by the emperor, emphasizing its authoritative status.
  • 四库全书 - The 'Four Treasuries of Books' is one of the largest compilations of Chinese literature, containing over 3,000 volumes and 79,000 separate works.
  • 子部 - One of the four major categories of the imperial compilation, alongside the 'Jing' (classics), 'Shi' (histories), and 'Ji' (literary collections).

Proper Nouns

  • 钦定 - Imperially designated, indicating official sanction.
  • 四库全书 - The Four Treasuries of Books.
  • 子部 - The Zi section of the compilation.
  • 十二 - The twelfth category.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 開天傳信記 - Title meaning 'Records of the Heavenly Era, Passed Down with Faith.'
  • 小説家二【異聞之屬】提要 - Classification as a type of 'novel' or 'storytelling' work, specifically belonging to the category of 'strange tales' or 'extraordinary events.'
  • 簿領之暇 - Literally 'in the spare time of dealing with official documents,' meaning during one's free time from administrative duties.
  • 捜求遺逸 - Means 'to seek out and gather lost or forgotten knowledge.'
  • 期於必信 - Means 'to aim for absolute credibility' or 'to ensure that the information is unquestionably true.'
  • 傳信 - The name of the text, meaning 'to pass down with faith' or 'to transmit with certainty.'
  • 歇後鄭五 - A playful nickname for Vang Qìng, referring to his habit of using cryptic or indirect speech in poetry.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 開天 - Refers to the reign of Emperor Xuanzong (Tianbao period, 713–756 CE), a time of prosperity and cultural flourishing in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 明皇 - A respectful title for Emperor Xuanzong, meaning 'Bright Emperor.'
  • 城南 - Refers to the southern part of Chang'an (modern-day Xi'an), the capital of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 王琚 - A key figure in the Tang court, known for his role in the coup that overthrew Empress Wei.
  • 韦氏 - Refers to the family of Empress Wei, who briefly seized power after the death of Emperor Zhongzong.
  • 太平公主 - A powerful princess who supported Empress Wei and played a significant role in the politics of the early Tang Dynasty.
  • 华隂 - A county in Shaanxi Province, home to Mount Hua, one of the Five Great Mountains of China.
  • 月宫 - The 'Moon Palace' is a mythical place in Chinese folklore, often associated with immortals and the moon goddess Chang'e.
  • 羅公逺 - A legendary Daoist immortal, known for his supernatural abilities.
  • 叶法善 - Another famous Daoist immortal, known for his mastery of magic.

Proper Nouns

  • 開天傳信記 - The title of the text.
  • 鄭綮 - The author of the text.
  • 唐 - The Tang Dynasty.
  • 昭宗 - Emperor Zhaozong, the last ruler of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 禮部侍郎 - A mid-level official in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 同中書門下平章事 - A title indicating membership in the imperial council.
  • 吏部員外郎 - A mid-level official in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 舊唐書 - The *Old History of the Tang Dynasty*, a historical record of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 明皇 - Emperor Xuanzong.
  • 王琚 - A key figure in the Tang court.
  • 韋氏 - The family of Empress Wei.
  • 太平公主 - A powerful princess who supported Empress Wei.
  • 華隂 - A county in Shaanxi Province.
  • 月宫 - The 'Moon Palace' in Chinese mythology.
  • 羅公逺 - A legendary Daoist immortal.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 累忝台郎 - Humble expression meaning 'repeatedly held the position of Lang,' indicating the translator's modest acknowledgment of his official rank.
  • 思勤坟典 - Literally 'think diligently about the classics,' referring to the translator's dedication to studying classical texts.
  • 用自修励 - Means 'to self-cultivate and encourage oneself,' expressing the translator's motivation for personal improvement.
  • 服膺简防 - Literally 'hold close the classics and laws,' indicating a deep respect and adherence to traditional teachings.
  • 管窥王业 - A humble expression meaning 'to view the state affairs through a narrow tube,' suggesting a limited but sincere interest in understanding governance.
  • 传于必信 - Means 'to transmit with certainty,' emphasizing the reliability of the information being conveyed.
  • 斗筲微器 - A humble self-reference, meaning 'a small and insignificant vessel,' used to express modesty.
  • 周鼎不节之咎 - Literally 'the fault of the Zhou tripod not being in harmony,' a metaphor for the translator's perceived inadequacy in comparison to the grandeur of the past.
  • 遐乎 - Means 'how far' or 'how distant,' often used to express a sense of distance or longing.
  • 好事者 - Refers to 'those who are fond of learning' or 'scholars.'
  • 观其志 - Means 'to observe their intentions' or 'to understand their motives.'
  • 是其心也 - Means 'to approve of their sincerity' or 'to recognize their genuine intentions.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 国朝 - Refers to the current dynasty, in this case, the Tang Dynasty.
  • 配享 - Refers to being included in the temple sacrifices, a high honor for officials and their contributions.
  • 藩邸 - Refers to the prince's residence or the place where the future emperor lived before ascending the throne.
  • 韦杜 - Refers to the regions of Wei and Du, which were prestigious areas in Chang'an (modern-day Xi'an).
  • 王琚 - A historical figure who played a significant role in the early life of Emperor Xuanzong and later became a trusted advisor.

Proper Nouns

  • 纪昀 - Ji Yun, a prominent scholar and official during the Qing Dynasty.
  • 陆锡熊 - Lu Xixiong, another scholar and official during the Qing Dynasty.
  • 孙士毅 - Sun Shiyi, a scholar and official during the Qing Dynasty.
  • 陆费墀 - Lu Feicheng, a scholar and official during the Qing Dynasty.
  • 郑綮 - Zheng Qi, the author of the text.
  • 王琚 - Wang Ju, a historical figure who played a significant role in the early life of Emperor Xuanzong.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 于诸王友爱特甚 - Showed extraordinary affection for his brothers; emphasizes the depth of the emperor's love.
  • 长枕与诸王同起卧 - A long pillow to sleep with his brothers; symbolizes close companionship.
  • 终日不食终夜不寝 - Stayed awake all night and went without food all day; indicates extreme concern.
  • 忧形于色 - Worries were visible on his face; describes the emperor's emotional state.
  • 左右或开谕进食 - Advisors tried to persuade him to eat; shows the attempts to calm him.
  • 手足不理吾身废矣 - If I don't take care of my brothers, I am neglecting myself; uses a metaphor to express the importance of family.
  • 防集宴乐之地上 - A place for gatherings and feasts; refers to a designated area for family activities.
  • 靡日不防聚 - They met almost every day; indicates frequent interactions.
  • 讲经义论理道 - Discussed Confucian teachings and principles; shows intellectual engagement.
  • 间以毬猎蒱博 - Engaged in ball games, hunting, and gambling; describes leisure activities.
  • 赋诗饮食欢笑戱谑 - Recited poetry, enjoyed meals, laughed, and joked; highlights the joyful atmosphere.
  • 近古帝王友爱之道无与比也 - The brotherly love of ancient emperors could not compare; emphasizes the uniqueness of the emperor's behavior.
  • 为譲者数四焉 - He repeatedly declined the throne; indicates humility and reluctance.
  • 献东封书 - Submitted a book on the eastern seal; refers to a document or treatise.
  • 命宰相出题就中书试验 - Ordered the chancellor to set questions and test him in the Secretariat; describes the examination process.
  • 亲为画眉总丱髻 - The emperor personally drew eyebrows and tied his hair; shows intimate care.
  • 投果遗花者不可胜数 - People threw fruits and flowers, too many to count; indicates widespread admiration.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 上 - Refers to Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, also known as Emperor Minghuang.
  • 诸王 - Refers to the emperor's brothers, including Crown Prince Li Chongjun and Prince Li Chongshi.
  • 东都 - Refers to Luoyang, the eastern capital of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 上都 - Refers to Chang'an, the western capital of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 花萼相辉之楼 - Refers to the Flower and Bud Pavilion, a famous building in Chang'an.
  • 开元初 - Refers to the early years of the Kaiyuan era, a period of prosperity under Emperor Xuanzong.
  • 安西诸国 - Refers to the Western Regions, areas under Tang control in Central Asia.
  • 开逺门 - Refers to the Kaiyuan Gate in Chang'an.
  • 河清海晏 - A phrase meaning 'clear rivers and calm seas,' symbolizing peace and prosperity.
  • 物殷俗阜 - Means 'abundant goods and prosperous customs,' describing economic prosperity.
  • 管户一千余万 - Refers to the population of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 米一斗三四文 - Means 'rice costs three or four coins per dou,' indicating low prices.
  • 丁壮之人不识兵器 - Means 'young men did not know what weapons were,' suggesting a peaceful society.
  • 路不拾遗 - Means 'no one picked up lost items on the road,' indicating a high level of trust and security.
  • 行者不囊粮 - Means 'travelers did not need to carry food,' suggesting abundance.
  • 重驿麕至 - Means 'messengers arrived frequently,' indicating good communication.
  • 刘晏 - Refers to Liu Yan, a prominent statesman and economist of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 东封书 - Refers to a book on the eastern seal, possibly a treatise on governance or philosophy.
  • 宰相 - Refers to the chancellor, the highest-ranking official in the imperial court.
  • 中书 - Refers to the Secretariat, one of the central government institutions.
  • 贵妃 - Refers to Yang Guifei, the favorite concubine of Emperor Xuanzong.
  • 总丱髻 - Means 'tied his hair in a topknot,' a hairstyle for young boys.

Proper Nouns

  • 上 - Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 诸王 - Emperor Xuanzong's brothers.
  • 东都 - Luoyang.
  • 上都 - Chang'an.
  • 花萼相辉之楼 - Flower and Bud Pavilion.
  • 开元初 - Early years of the Kaiyuan era.
  • 安西诸国 - Western Regions.
  • 开逺门 - Kaiyuan Gate.
  • 河清海晏 - Clear rivers and calm seas.
  • 物殷俗阜 - Abundant goods and prosperous customs.
  • 管户一千余万 - Population of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 米一斗三四文 - Cost of rice.
  • 丁壮之人不识兵器 - Young men not knowing weapons.
  • 路不拾遗 - No one picking up lost items.
  • 行者不囊粮 - Travelers not carrying food.
  • 重驿麕至 - Messengers arriving frequently.
  • 刘晏 - Liu Yan.
  • 东封书 - Book on the eastern seal.
  • 宰相 - Chancellor.
  • 中书 - Secretariat.
  • 贵妃 - Yang Guifei.
  • 总丱髻 - Tied his hair in a topknot.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 开元初 - In the early years of Kaiyuan, referring to the reign of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 山东大蝗 - A great locust plague in Shandong.
  • 分遣使 - To dispatch officials or send out envoys.
  • 得无违而伤义乎 - Rhetorical question asking if the action is contrary to moral principles.
  • 秉畀炎火 - Direct quote from the *Dàyán* (Book of Documents), a method of pest control.
  • 安农 - Protecting agriculture or ensuring agricultural stability.
  • 除害 - Eliminating pests.
  • 兴农去害 - Promoting agriculture and eliminating pests.
  • 幸陛下熟思之 - Hope Your Majesty will carefully consider this.
  • 事既师古用可救时 - If we follow ancient practices, we can save the times.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 开元 - Reign period of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, known for cultural and economic prosperity.
  • 姚元崇 - Yao Yuanzong, a prominent statesman and chancellor during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 山东 - Historically significant region for its agricultural production.
  • 秉畀炎火 - Phrase from the *Dàyán* (Book of Documents), one of the Five Classics of Confucianism.
  • 安农 - Protecting agriculture, a fundamental concern in Chinese governance.
  • 除害 - Eliminating pests, a common agricultural practice.
  • 兴农去害 - Promoting agriculture and eliminating pests, crucial for national stability.

Proper Nouns

  • 开元 - Reign period of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 姚元崇 - Yao Yuanzong, a prominent statesman and chancellor during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 山东 - The Shandong region.
  • 秉畀炎火 - Phrase from the *Dàyán* (Book of Documents).
  • 安农 - Protecting agriculture.
  • 除害 - Eliminating pests.
  • 兴农去害 - Promoting agriculture and eliminating pests.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 上将登封泰山 - The emperor ascended Mount Tai to perform the ritual of Fengshan, a significant imperial ceremony.
  • 益州进白骡至洁朗丰润权竒伟异 - A white mule from Yizhou arrived, clean, bright, and of extraordinary beauty, describing the mule's qualities.
  • 柔习安便 - It was gentle, well-trained, and comfortable, describing the mule's temperament and training.
  • 不知登降之倦 - It did not show any signs of fatigue from ascending and descending, emphasizing the mule's endurance.
  • 才下山坳休息未乆 - As soon as it reached the bottom of the hill, it rested for a short while, describing the mule's immediate need for rest.
  • 有司言白骡无疾而殪 - Officials reported that the white mule died without any apparent illness, indicating an unexpected death.
  • 上叹异之 - The emperor was amazed and saddened by this, expressing the emperor's reaction.
  • 谥曰白骡将军 - It was posthumously given the title of General White Mule, a formal honorific.
  • 命有司具槥椟叠石为墓 - The emperor ordered officials to prepare a coffin and stone burial mound, detailing the burial arrangements.
  • 在封禅坛北一里余 - It was located about one mile north of the Fengshan altar, providing a specific location.
  • 于今在焉 - It still exists today, indicating the current state of the site.
  • 车驾次华隂 - The imperial entourage arrived at Huayin, describing the location of the event.
  • 上见岳神数里迎谒 - The emperor saw the mountain god several miles away, welcoming him, describing a supernatural encounter.
  • 上问左右莫之见 - The emperor asked those around him, but none saw anything, indicating the emperor's perception of the event.
  • 遂诏诸巫问神安在 - He then summoned various shamans to ask where the god was, showing the emperor's response.
  • 独老巫阿马婆奏云三郎在路左朱发紫衣迎陛下 - Only the old shaman Amapo reported that the Third Prince was on the left side of the road, wearing red hair and purple clothes, waiting for the emperor, describing the shaman's vision.
  • 上顾笑之 - The emperor smiled at this, indicating his reaction to the shaman's report.
  • 仍勑阿马婆勑神先归 - He then ordered Amapo to tell the god to return first, showing the emperor's instructions.
  • 上至庙见神櫜鞬俯伏庭东南大栢树下 - When the emperor arrived at the temple, he saw the god, with his bow and arrows, kneeling under a large pine tree in the southeast corner of the courtyard, describing another supernatural encounter.
  • 又召阿马婆问之对如上见 - He then summoned Amapo again to ask, and she responded as she had seen, confirming the shaman's vision.
  • 上加敬礼命阿马婆致意而旋 - The emperor added more respect and instructed Amapo to convey his intentions and then returned, showing the emperor's actions.
  • 降诏先诣岳封为金天王 - He issued an edict to first visit the mountain and declare it the King of Gold Heaven, indicating a formal declaration.
  • 仍上自书制碑文以宠异之 - He also wrote the inscription for the stele himself to honor and distinguish it, showing the emperor's personal involvement.
  • 其碑髙五十余尺濶丈余厚四五尺 - The stele was over fifty feet tall, more than ten feet wide, and four to five feet thick, providing specific measurements.
  • 天下碑莫比也 - No other stele in the world could compare to it, emphasizing its grandeur.
  • 其余刻扈从太子王公以下百官名氏制作壮丽巧无比伦焉 - The rest of the stele carved the names of the imperial entourage, princes, nobles, and officials, and the design was magnificent and intricate, unmatched in the world, describing the stele's detailed content.
  • 上为皇孙时风表瓌异神采英迈 - When he was a prince, he had a striking appearance and extraordinary spirit, describing the emperor's youthful qualities.
  • 尝于朝堂叱武攸暨曰朝堂我家朝堂汝得恣蜂虿而狼顾耶 - He once scolded Wu Youji in the court, saying, 'This court is my family's court. How dare you act like a scorpion and look around suspiciously?' showing the emperor's authority and demeanor.
  • 则天闻 - Empress Wu heard this, indicating the involvement of Empress Wu.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 登封泰山 - The Fengshan ceremony was a significant ritual performed by emperors to communicate with heaven and earth.
  • 益州 - A historical region in southwestern China, known for its rich resources and strategic importance.
  • 白骡 - A white mule, often symbolizing purity and nobility.
  • 岳神 - The mountain god, a deity associated with Mount Tai.
  • 阿马婆 - An old shaman, a religious figure who communicates with deities.
  • 三郎 - The Third Prince, a title often given to a younger son of an emperor.
  • 金天王 - The King of Gold Heaven, a title given to the mountain god.
  • 则天 - Empress Wu Zetian, a powerful and influential empress regnant during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 朝堂 - The imperial court, the place where the emperor and his officials conducted official business.

Proper Nouns

  • 上 - The emperor, a title used to refer to the ruler.
  • 益州 - A historical region in southwestern China.
  • 白骡 - A white mule.
  • 岳神 - The mountain god.
  • 阿马婆 - An old shaman.
  • 三郎 - The Third Prince.
  • 金天王 - The King of Gold Heaven.
  • 则天 - Empress Wu Zetian.
  • 朝堂 - The imperial court.
  • 武攸暨 - A person mentioned in the text, likely a court official.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 驚異之再三顧 - Surprised and looked at repeatedly, indicating a strong reaction and repeated observation.
  • 此兒氣概終當為吾家太平天子也 - This child's demeanor will surely make him our family's peaceful emperor, suggesting a prediction of future greatness.
  • 西涼州習好音樂 - The people of Western Liang state are fond of music, describing a cultural characteristic.
  • 制新曲曰涼州 - Created a new tune called 'Liangzhou', referring to the creation of a musical piece.
  • 開元中列上獻上 - During the Kaiyuan era, it was presented to the emperor, indicating a historical event.
  • 召諸王便殿同觀 - Summoned the princes to a side hall to watch together, describing a gathering.
  • 曲終諸王賀舞蹈稱善 - At the end of the song, the princes congratulated and danced, praising it, showing a positive response.
  • 獨寧王不拜 - Only Prince Ning did not bow, indicating a unique reaction.
  • 上顧問之 - The emperor asked him, showing the emperor's curiosity.
  • 此曲雖嘉臣有聞焉 - This song is indeed beautiful, but I have heard something about it, expressing a mixed opinion.
  • 夫音者始于宮散于商成于角征羽 - Music starts with the palace tone, spreads to the commercial tone, and completes in the angular, Zheng, and Yu tones, explaining the structure of music.
  • 斯曲也宮離而少征商亂而加暴 - This song has a weak palace tone and too much commercial tone, which is chaotic and violent, criticizing the music.
  • 臣聞宮君也商臣也 - I have heard that the palace tone represents the ruler and the commercial tone represents the minister, explaining the symbolic meaning of tones.
  • 宮不勝則君勢卑商有余則臣事僣卑則逼下僣則犯上 - If the palace tone is weak, the ruler's power is low; if the commercial tone is excessive, the minister's actions are僭越, which is disrespectful to the ruler, warning of the consequences.
  • 發于忽微形于音聲播于歌咏見之于人事 - It starts from subtle signs, manifests in sounds, and appears in songs and people's lives, explaining the impact of music.
  • 臣恐一日有播越之祸悖逼之患 - I fear that one day it will lead to rebellion and disorder, expressing concern.
  • 上聞之黙然 - The emperor listened silently, showing his reaction.
  • 及安史作乱华夏鼎沸 - When the An Lushan and Shi Siming rebellion occurred, the whole country was in chaos, referring to a historical event.
  • 所以見寧王審音之妙也 - This shows Prince Ning's keen understanding of music, praising his insight.
  • 天寳中上以三河道險束漕运艰难 - During the Tianbao era, the emperor faced difficulties in transporting grain due to dangerous river routes, describing a historical problem.
  • 乃傍北山凿石为月河以避湍急 - So he ordered the construction of a stone channel along the north mountain to avoid the rapid currents, describing a solution.
  • 名曰天寳河 - It was named the Tianbao River, giving the project a name.
  • 歲省运夫五十万人 - It saved fifty thousand laborers each year, quantifying the benefit.
  • 无覆溺淹滞之患 - There was no risk of drowning or delays, describing the safety and efficiency.
  • 天下称之 - It was praised throughout the land, indicating widespread recognition.
  • 其河东西径直长五里余濶四五丈深三四丈 - The river is about five miles long, four to five zhang wide, and three to four zhang deep, providing specific measurements.
  • 皆凿坚石 - It was all carved from hard stone, describing the construction.
  • 匠人于石得古铁鍱长三尺余上有平陆二字皆文也 - The craftsmen found an ancient iron plate in the stone, three chi long, with the words 'Pinglu' in ancient script, describing a discovery.
  • 上异之蔵于内库 - The emperor was surprised and kept it in the inner treasury, showing his reaction.
  • 遂命改河北县为平陆县旌其事也 - He then ordered the renaming of Hebei county to Pinglu county to commemorate the event, describing the action taken.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 西涼州 - Refers to the Western Liang state, a region known for its musical culture.
  • 涼州 - The name of the new tune created.
  • 開元中 - Refers to the Kaiyuan era, a period of prosperity under Emperor Xuanzong.
  • 寧王 - Prince Ning, a member of the imperial family.
  • 天寳中 - Refers to the Tianbao era, another period of prosperity under Emperor Xuanzong.
  • 三河道 - The three major rivers (Yellow River, Huai River, and Yangtze River) that posed transportation challenges.
  • 月河 - A stone channel constructed to improve transportation.
  • 平陆 - The name given to the new county, commemorating the discovery of the ancient iron plate.

Proper Nouns

  • 寧王 - Prince Ning.

[8]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 大酺 - A large-scale feast or celebration, often held by the emperor.
  • 百戯 - A variety of entertainments or acrobatic shows.
  • 犯此者死 - Anyone who violates this rule will be executed.
  • 学问日新 - Knowledge becomes increasingly new and advanced daily.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 勤政楼 - The Qinzhi Tower, a famous building in Chang'an (modern-day Xi'an) during the Tang Dynasty, used for official ceremonies and celebrations.
  • 昆仑奴 - A type of slave, often of African descent, commonly mentioned in Tang Dynasty literature.
  • 严安之 - A historical figure, likely a court official or advisor.
  • 平内难 - Refers to the suppression of internal rebellions or uprisings, a common theme in Tang Dynasty history.
  • 制诏络绎 - Refers to the frequent issuance of imperial edicts, indicating a period of active governance.

Proper Nouns

  • 勤政楼 - The Qinzhi Tower.
  • 昆仑奴 - A type of slave.
  • 严安之 - A historical figure.
  • 许公 - A title for a high-ranking official.

[9]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 上封泰山 - Refers to the act of performing a ritual at Mount Tai, a significant mountain in Chinese tradition associated with imperial ceremonies.
  • 回车驾次上党路 - Describes the emperor's journey, where he turns his chariot and proceeds along the road to Shanxi Province (Shangdang).
  • 父老担壷浆逺近迎谒 - Describes the elderly people carrying loads and offering drinks, coming from far and near to greet the emperor.
  • 上皆亲加存问受其献馈锡赉有差 - The emperor personally greets, asks about their well-being, accepts their gifts, and rewards them according to their status.
  • 父老有先与上相识者 - Some of the elderly people were previously acquainted with the emperor.
  • 上悉赐酒食与之话旧故 - The emperor gives them wine and food and talks about old times with them.
  • 过村部必令询访孤老丧疾之家加吊恤之 - Whenever passing through villages, the emperor orders inquiries into the homes of widows, the elderly, and those who are sick or bereaved, and extends condolences and assistance.
  • 父老忻忻然莫不瞻戴叩乞驻留焉 - The elderly people are delighted and all look up to him, begging him to stay.
  • 车金桥御路萦转 - The golden bridge and imperial road wind and turn.
  • 旌纛鲜洁羽卫整齐 - The banners and flags are bright and clean, and the guard is well-organized.
  • 顾谓左右曰 - The emperor turns and says to those around him.
  • 遂诏呉道韦无忝陈闳令同制金桥图圣容及上所乗照夜白马陈闳主之桥梁山水车舆人物草树鴈鸟器仗帷幕呉道主之狗马骡驴牛羊骆驼猫猴猪四足之韦无忝主之图成时为三絶焉 - The emperor then ordered Wu Daoxuan, Wei Wutian, and Chen Hong to jointly create a painting of the golden bridge, the emperor's face, and his horse that shines at night. Chen Hong was in charge of the bridge, mountains, rivers, vehicles, people, trees, birds, utensils, and curtains. Wu Daoxuan was in charge of dogs, horses, donkeys, mules, cattle, sheep, camels, monkeys, pigs, and other four-legged animals. When the painting was completed, it was considered a 'triple marvel.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 封泰山 - A ritual performed by emperors to show their connection with heaven and earth, often involving sacrifices and offerings.
  • 上党 - A historical region in Shanxi Province, known for its strategic importance.
  • 父老 - Refers to the elderly people, often respected members of the community.
  • 张説 - Zhang Yue, a prominent statesman and poet during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 后土碑 - The Later Earth Stele, a significant historical artifact.
  • 韦无忝 - Wei Wutian, another notable painter of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 陈闳 - Chen Hong, a painter known for his landscapes and figures.

Proper Nouns

  • 上 - The emperor.
  • 张説 - Zhang Yue.
  • 后土碑 - The Later Earth Stele.
  • 韦无忝 - Wei Wutian.
  • 陈闳 - Chen Hong.
  • 泰山 - Mount Tai.
  • 上党 - Shanxi Province.
  • 陜右 - The western part of Shanxi Province.
  • 太原 - Taiyuan, a city in Shanxi Province.

[10]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 上幸蜀 - The emperor traveled to Shu; '幸' indicates a royal visit.
  • 車駕次劔門 - The imperial entourage arrived at the Sword Gate; '次' means to stay or arrive.
  • 門左右岩壁峭絶 - The rocks on both sides of the gate were steep and precipitous; '峭絶' describes extreme steepness.
  • 敗亡相继 - Successions of defeats and losses; '相继' means in succession.
  • 因驻跸题诗 - The emperor paused to compose a poem; '驻跸' means to stop and stay.
  • 銮舆出狩回 - The imperial carriage returned from the hunt; '出狩' means to go hunting.
  • 翠屏千仞合 - The green screens of a thousand feet converged; '翠屏' refers to green mountains or cliffs.
  • 丹障五丁开 - The red barriers were opened by the Five Dukes; '丹障' refers to red cliffs.
  • 灌木萦旗转 - The flags turned as they passed through the dense vegetation; '灌木' means dense vegetation.
  • 嗟尔勒铭才 - Alas, you have the talent to inscribe; '嗟尔' is an expression of pity or sorrow.
  • 上何谑我耶 - Why is the emperor joking with me?; '谑' means to joke or tease.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 贾深 - A local official during Emperor Dezong's reign, who inscribed the emperor's poem on a stone wall in 758 CE.
  • 贺知章 - A prominent poet and statesman of the Tang dynasty, known for his high reputation and eventual retirement to his hometown in Wu (modern-day Zhejiang).
  • 孚 - A name given to a son by Emperor Dezong, which was later interpreted as a pun in Wu dialect.

Proper Nouns

  • 贾深 - A local official.
  • 贺知章 - A prominent poet and statesman.
  • 孚 - A name given to a son by Emperor Dezong.

[11]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 以手指上下按其腹 - Ambiguous phrase; likely means 'to gesture with fingers on the stomach' in context.
  • 夢遊月宮 - Idiom for a dream journey to the Moon Palace, a place of celestial beings.
  • 上清之樂 - Music of the highest purity, associated with immortals.
  • 以玉笛尋之 - To seek it out with a jade flute, implying recreating the music heard in a dream.
  • 紫云回 - Name of the tune, humorously revealed as 'Purple Cloud Returns'.
  • 封泰山 - Ritual of ascending Mount Tai, symbolizing the emperor's connection to heaven.
  • 命弧矢射之 - Ordered the bow and arrow to shoot at it.
  • 賞望嘉其髙迥 - To enjoy the view and admire its height and remoteness.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 上 - Refers to Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, a prominent ruler known for his cultural achievements.
  • 月宮 - The Moon Palace, a place of celestial beings in Chinese mythology.
  • 玉笛 - A jade flute, a symbol of refined and noble music in Chinese culture.
  • 開元 - The beginning of the Kaiyuan era, a period of great cultural and economic prosperity.
  • 華岳 - Mount Hua, one of the Five Great Mountains, known for its spiritual significance.
  • 云台觀 - A famous Taoist temple, often associated with immortals and spiritual practices.

Proper Nouns

  • 髙力士 - Gao Lishi, a prominent eunuch and close advisor to Emperor Xuanzong.
  • 荥阳 - A city in Henan Province, historically significant for its strategic location.
  • 旃然河 - The Zhanran River, a tributary of the Yellow River.
  • 瓮肚峯 - Wengdu Peak, a mountain on Mount Hua.

[12]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 識者解之 - '識者' (shizhe) means 'those who understand' or 'interpreters.' The phrase indicates that knowledgeable people interpret the symbol.
  • 得寳之年遂改天寳也 - '得寳之年' (debaonian) means 'the year of obtaining the treasure,' and '遂改天寳' (suigai tianbao) means 'thus changed to the era name Tianbao.' This indicates that the discovery of the treasure led to a change in the era name.
  • 上于农古函谷闗得寳符 - '上' (shang) refers to the emperor, and '农古函谷闗' (Hongnong Guguan) is the ancient Hangu Pass in Hongnong. The phrase describes the emperor's discovery of the treasure symbol at this location.
  • 上幸爱禄山为子 - '上幸爱' (shangxing'ai) means 'the emperor favored,' and '禄山为子' (Lushan wei zi) means 'Lushan as his son.' This indicates that the emperor treated An Lushan like a son.
  • 每就坐不拜上而拜妃 - '每就坐' (mei jiu zuo) means 'every time he sits down,' '不拜上' (bu bai shang) means 'does not bow to the emperor,' and '拜妃' (bai fei) means 'bows to the empress.' This describes An Lushan's behavior towards the emperor and empress.
  • 此胡不拜我而拜妃子意何在也 - '此胡' (ci hu) means 'this man' (referring to An Lushan), '不拜我而拜妃' (bu bai wo er bai fei) means 'does not bow to me but bows to the empress,' and '意何在也' (yi he zai ye) means 'what is his intention?' This is the emperor's question to An Lushan.
  • 胡家即知有母不知有父故也 - '胡家即知有母不知有父' (hu jia ji zhi you mu bu zhi you fu) means 'in the triracial barbarian's culture, they know of a mother but not a father,' and '故也' (gu ye) means 'therefore.' This is An Lushan's explanation.
  • 上笑而舍之 - '上笑而舍之' (shang xiao er she zhi) means 'the emperor laughed and let it go.' This indicates the emperor's reaction to An Lushan's explanation.
  • 一行将卒留物一封命弟子进于上 - '一行将卒' (yixing jiang zu) means 'when Yi Xing was about to die,' '留物一封' (liu wu yi feng) means 'left a sealed package,' and '命弟子进于上' (ming disi jin yu shang) means 'ordered his disciples to present it to the emperor.' This describes Yi Xing's final act.
  • 发而视之乃蜀当归也 - '发而视之' (fa er shi zhi) means 'opened and saw,' and '乃蜀当归也' (nai shu danggui ye) means 'it was Sichuan Angelica root.' This describes the contents of the package.
  • 上初不谕及幸蜀回乃知微防深叹异之 - '上初不谕' (shang chu bu yu) means 'the emperor initially did not understand,' '及幸蜀回' (ji xing shu hui) means 'when he visited Sichuan and returned,' and '乃知微防深叹异之' (nai zhi wei fang shen tan yi zhi) means 'then realized the subtle meaning and deeply admired it.' This describes the emperor's realization.
  • 罗公逺多秘术最善形之法 - '罗公逺多秘术' (luo gong yuan duo mi shu) means 'Lu Gongyuan had many secret arts,' and '最善形之法' (zui shan xing xing zhi fa) means 'was most skilled in the art of transforming into different forms.' This describes Lu Gongyuan's abilities.
  • 上就公逺虽传受不肯尽其要 - '上就公逺' (shang jiu gong yuan) means 'the emperor studied with Lu Gongyuan,' '虽传受' (sui chuan shou) means 'even though he taught and received,' and '不肯尽其要' (bu ken jin qi yao) means 'would not fully reveal its essence.' This describes the emperor's learning process.
  • 上毎与同为之则没人不能知 - '上毎与同为之' (shang mei yu tong wei zhi) means 'every time the emperor practiced with him,' '则没人不能知' (ze xing mei ren bu neng zhi) means 'no one could see the transformation.' This describes the effect of the transformation.
  • 若自试或余衣带防露幞头脚每被宫人知上所在 - '若自试' (ruo zi shi) means 'if he tried on his own,' '或余衣带防露幞头脚' (huo yu yi dai fang lu fu tou jiao) means 'or if his clothes, belt, or headband were visible,' and '每被宫人知上所在' (mei bei gong ren zhi shang suozai) means 'the palace women always knew where the emperor was.' This describes the limitations of the transformation.
  • 上怒命力士裹以油幞置榨木下压杀而埋弃之 - '上怒' (shang nu) means 'the emperor was angry,' '命力士裹以油幞' (ming li shi guo yi you fu) means 'ordered a strongman to wrap him in oil-soaked cloth,' '置榨木下压杀' (zhi zha mu xia ya sha) means 'place him under a press and crush him to death,' and '而埋弃之' (er mai qi zhi) means 'and bury him.' This describes the emperor's punishment.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 一行 - A famous monk and astronomer of the Tang Dynasty, known for his contributions to science and his close relationship with the emperor.
  • 罗公逺 - A Daoist priest and alchemist known for his magical abilities and close association with the emperor.

[13]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 万回师阌郷人也 - Introduces Wan Hui as a character and his place of origin.
  • 神用不足谓愚而痴 - Describes Wan Hui's perceived lack of intelligence.
  • 无所知虽父母亦以豚犬畜之 - Emphasizes the low regard in which Wan Hui is held by his family.
  • 兄被戍役安西音问隔絶 - Explains the separation and lack of communication between Wan Hui and his brother.
  • 父母谓其诚死日夕涕泣而忧思也 - Highlights the emotional state of Wan Hui's parents.
  • 万回顾父感念甚忽跪而言曰 - Describes Wan Hui's sudden change in behavior and his emotional state.
  • 涕泣岂非忧兄也 - Wan Hui's question to his father about his concern for his brother.
  • 父母曰信然 - Confirms the parents' concern for Wan Hui's brother.
  • 详思我兄所要者衣装糗粮巾之属悉备之某将觐焉 - Describes Wan Hui's actions and intentions.
  • 忽一日朝赍所备夕返其家 - Describes Wan Hui's miraculous journey.
  • 告父母曰兄平善矣 - Confirms Wan Hui's success in finding his brother.
  • 发书视之乃兄迹也 - Describes the evidence of Wan Hui's success.
  • 一家异之 - Highlights the family's reaction to Wan Hui's actions.
  • 居常貌如愚痴忽有先觉异见惊人神异也 - Describes Wan Hui's dual nature.
  • 上在藩邸或游行人间 - Describes Wan Hui's behavior when the emperor is present.
  • 天子来或曰圣人来 - Describes Wan Hui's predictions.
  • 其处信宿间上必经过徘徊也 - Confirms the accuracy of Wan Hui's predictions.
  • 安乐公主上之季妹也 - Introduces Anle Princess.
  • 道路惧焉 - Highlights the fear surrounding Anle Princess.
  • 万回望其车骑道唾曰 - Describes Wan Hui's reaction to Anle Princess.
  • 血腥不可近也不旋踵而灭亡之祸及矣 - Wan Hui's prediction about Anle Princess.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 万回 - A historical figure known for his supernatural abilities and predictions.
  • 安西 - A region in Central Asia where the Tang Dynasty had military presence.
  • 安乐公主 - A historical figure, the youngest sister of Emperor Zhongzong of Tang.
  • 韦氏 - Refers to the powerful Wei family during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 集贤院 - The Academy of Literature, an institution for scholars and literary works.

Proper Nouns

  • 万回 - A historical figure known for his supernatural abilities.
  • 安西 - A region in Central Asia.
  • 安乐公主 - A historical figure, the youngest sister of Emperor Zhongzong of Tang.
  • 韦氏 - The powerful Wei family during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 集贤院 - The Academy of Literature.

[14]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 精于符箓之术 - Excelling in the art of talismanic magic, where '符箓' refers to talismans or magical scripts used in Taoist rituals.
  • 鸿胪卿 - Minister of Ceremonies, an official title in the imperial court responsible for foreign affairs and ceremonies.
  • 优礼待焉 - Was treated with great respect, indicating a high level of honor.
  • 朝客数十人诣之 - Dozens of court officials visited him, where '朝客' refers to officials from the imperial court.
  • 解带淹留满座思酒 - They relaxed and stayed, thinking about wine, indicating a casual and enjoyable atmosphere.
  • 麴秀才 - Mr. Qu, a respectful term for a scholar or literatus.
  • 方有朝僚未暇瞻晤 - I have court officials here and cannot spare the time to greet you, indicating a polite refusal.
  • 异日见临也 - Please visit another time, a polite way to invite someone back.
  • 美措傲睨而入 - A handsome young man entered with a haughty look, describing the demeanor of the guest.
  • 抗声谈论 - Spoke loudly and confidently, indicating a bold and assertive manner.
  • 援引古人 - Cited ancient examples, referring to the use of historical or literary references in conversation.
  • 一席不测恐耸观之 - The entire audience was astonished and fearful, indicating the impact of the guest's speech.
  • 蹔起旋转 - He suddenly stood up and turned around, describing a sudden and unexpected action.
  • 魃魅为惑 - Possessed by a demon or ghost, a reference to supernatural interference.
  • 试与诸公避之 - Let's try to avoid him, suggesting a plan to evade the guest.
  • 扼腕抵掌 - Clasping his wrists and striking his palms, a gesture indicating intense emotion or argument.
  • 论难锋起 - The debate became sharp and intense, describing the heated nature of the discussion.
  • 势不可当 - His momentum was unstoppable, indicating the overwhelming force of the guest's argument.
  • 随手失坠于阶下 - He fell from my hand and dropped to the ground, describing the physical manifestation of the guest's supernatural nature.
  • 化为瓶榼 - Transformed into a jar of wine, indicating the supernatural transformation.
  • 座客醉而揖其瓶 - The guests drank the wine and bowed to the bottle, indicating their acceptance of the supernatural event.
  • 麴生风味不可忘也 - The taste of Qu is unforgettable, a poetic way of remembering the guest.
  • 性好释氏 - He had a fondness for Buddhism, indicating his religious inclinations.
  • 师事普寂禅师 - He studied under Master Puji, indicating his spiritual mentor.
  • 旦夕造谒焉 - He visited him every day and night, indicating frequent and devoted visits.
  • 屏賔从止于空室 - He dismissed his attendants and stayed in an empty room, indicating a private and secluded setting.
  • 洁涤正堂焚香端坐 - He cleaned the main hall, burned incense, and sat upright, indicating a ritualistic and meditative state.
  • 扣门连声云一行天师至 - He heard continuous knocking and said that Master Yin had arrived, indicating the arrival of a spiritual figure.
  • 作礼礼寂之足 - He bowed and touched Master Puji's feet, indicating a sign of respect and humility.
  • 无不可者 - Nothing is impossible, indicating a positive and open-minded attitude.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 道士 - Taoist priest, a religious figure in Chinese tradition.
  • 符箓之术 - Talismanic magic, a form of Taoist ritual practice.
  • 鸿胪卿 - Minister of Ceremonies, an important official in the imperial court.
  • 朝客 - Court officials, high-ranking government officials.
  • 麴秀才 - Mr. Qu, a respectful term for a scholar or literatus.
  • 普寂禅师 - Master Puji, a famous Buddhist monk.
  • 一行天师 - Master Yin, a spiritual figure, possibly a Taoist or Buddhist master.
  • 河南尹 - Governor of Henan, an official position in the imperial administration.
  • 释氏 - Buddhism, the religion associated with the teachings of the Buddha.

Proper Nouns

  • 叶法善 - Yè Fashàn, the name of the Taoist priest.
  • 麴秀才 - Qu Xiùcái, the name of the mysterious guest.
  • 普寂禅师 - Master Puji, the name of the Buddhist monk.
  • 一行天师 - Master Yin, the name of the spiritual figure.

[15]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 灭度矣 - Buddhist term meaning 'attained Nirvana' or 'passed away,' often used to describe the death of a monk or enlightened being.
  • 左右疾走视之 - Ran to look at it, describing a hurried action, likely in response to something unexpected or significant.
  • 一如其言 - Just as he said, indicating that something happened exactly as predicted or described.
  • 后寂灭度 - Later, he passed away, another Buddhist term for death, emphasizing the peaceful nature of the passing.
  • 寛复衰绖葬之 - Kuan, wearing mourning clothes, buried him, describing a formal burial ritual.
  • 徒步出城送之 - Walked out of the city to see him off, indicating a personal and respectful farewell.
  • 甚为缙绅所讥也 - Was heavily criticized by the gentry, suggesting that Kuan's actions were seen as improper or excessive by the elite.
  • 寛子谞复为河南尹 - Kuan's son, Xu, was again appointed as the governor of Henan, indicating a position of authority.
  • 素好谈谐 - Always enjoyed humorous conversation, describing Xu's personality.
  • 多异笔 - Often wrote unusual or eccentric things, indicating a creative or unconventional writing style.
  • 尝有投牒误书 - Once, someone submitted a petition with a mistake, describing a bureaucratic error.
  • 者畔似那畔 - Those on the other side seem to be on that side, a confusing or ambiguous phrase, possibly indicating a miscommunication or misunderstanding.
  • 猫儿不识主 - The cat doesn't recognize its owner, a metaphor for someone being unresponsive or uncooperative.
  • 傍我搦老防 - Beside me, grabbing the old guard, a phrase that is difficult to interpret, possibly referring to a physical action or a metaphorical situation.
  • 两家不须争 - The two families don't need to fight, suggesting a resolution to a conflict.
  • 遂纳其猫儿 - So he accepted the cat, indicating a decision to take responsibility for the cat.
  • 争者亦哂 - The disputants also laughed, suggesting that the resolution was seen as humorous or ironic.
  • 安禄山初为张韩公帐下走使之吏 - An Lushan initially served as a messenger for Zhang Han, a high-ranking official, describing An Lushan's early career.
  • 韩常令禄山洗足 - Han ordered An Lushan to wash his feet, a common act of hospitality or service.
  • 韩公脚下有黒防子 - Han had a black mole on his foot, a physical detail that may have been significant.
  • 禄山因洗脚而窃窥之 - An Lushan secretly looked at it while washing his feet, indicating a moment of curiosity or opportunism.
  • 韩公顾笑曰 - Han smiled and said, describing a moment of amusement or recognition.
  • 黒子吾贵相也 - The mole is a sign of my nobility, Han's interpretation of the mole as a symbol of his status.
  • 独汝窥之亦能有之乎 - Only you looked at it, could you also have one? Han's question to An Lushan, suggesting a challenge or test.
  • 某贱人也 - I am a lowly person, An Lushan's self-deprecating response.
  • 不幸两足皆有比将军者黒而加大 - Unfortunately, both my feet have moles larger than yours, An Lushan's revelation, which impressed Han.
  • 竟不知是何祥也 - I still don't know what it means, Han's admission of uncertainty.
  • 韩公竒而观之益亲厚之 - Han was amazed and treated him more favorably, indicating a change in Han's attitude toward An Lushan.
  • 约为义儿而加荐宠焉 - They agreed to become adoptive father and son, and Han recommended and favored An Lushan, describing the development of their relationship.
  • 无畏三蔵自天竺至 - Venerable Bodhiruci arrived from India, describing the arrival of a Buddhist monk.
  • 所由引谒上见而敬信焉 - He was introduced to the emperor, who respected and trusted him, indicating the monk's reception at court.
  • 臣在天竺国时闻西明寺宣律师持律第一愿依止焉 - When I was in India, I heard that Ximing Temple's Master Xuan has the highest standards of precepts, and I wish to stay with him, the monk's request.
  • 上可之 - The emperor agreed, indicating approval of the monk's request.
  • 宣律禁诫坚苦焚修精洁 - Master Xuan strictly adhered to precepts, practiced asceticism, and maintained purity, describing his religious discipline.
  • 三蔵饮酒食肉言行麤易 - Venerable Bodhiruci drank alcohol, ate meat, and spoke casually, indicating a contrast to Master Xuan's strictness.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 灭度 - Buddhist term for death, often used to describe the passing of an enlightened being.
  • 衰绖 - Mourning clothes, indicating a formal funeral ritual.
  • 缙绅 - Refers to the gentry or elite class, often used to describe the upper echelons of society.
  • 河南尹 - The governor of Henan, a high-ranking official in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 安禄山 - A powerful military leader and rebel during the Tang Dynasty, known for his role in the An Lushan Rebellion.
  • 张韩公 - A high-ranking official during the Tang Dynasty, under whom An Lushan initially served.
  • 韩公 - Another term for Zhang Han, emphasizing his status.
  • 无畏三蔵 - Venerable Bodhiruci, a Buddhist monk who traveled from India to China during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 西明寺 - A Buddhist temple in Chang'an, known for its strict adherence to precepts.
  • 宣律师 - Master Xuan, a Buddhist monk known for his strict discipline and high standards of precepts.

Proper Nouns

  • 安禄山 - An Lushan, a powerful military leader and rebel during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 张韩公 - Zhang Han, a high-ranking official during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 韩公 - Another term for Zhang Han, emphasizing his status.
  • 无畏三蔵 - Venerable Bodhiruci, a Buddhist monk who traveled from India to China during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 西明寺 - A Buddhist temple in Chang'an, known for its strict adherence to precepts.
  • 宣律师 - Master Xuan, a Buddhist monk known for his strict discipline and high standards of precepts.

[16]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 徃往乗醉而喧秽汚絪席 - Describes a scene where someone is often drunk and noisy, defiling the bedding. Captures both physical and moral defilement.
  • 宣律颇不甘心 - Indicates that the monk Xuanlü was quite dissatisfied or resentful.
  • 忽中夜宣律扪虱将投于地 - Describes a sudden event in the middle of the night where Xuanlü was picking lice from his body and was about to throw them away.
  • 三蔵半醉连声呼曰 - Indicates that the Three Treasures (Buddha, Dharma, Sangha) were half-drunk and called out repeatedly.
  • 扑杀佛子 - Means 'to kill the Buddha's son,' a severe insult.
  • 整衣作礼投而师事之 - Describes the act of straightening one's clothes, performing a bow, and treating someone as a teacher.
  • 宣律精苦之甚 - Indicates that Xuanlü was extremely diligent and hardworking.
  • 常夜行道临堦坠堕 - Describes a situation where Xuanlü often fell while walking at night.
  • 忽觉有人捧承其足 - Indicates that Xuanlü suddenly felt someone supporting his feet.
  • 宣律顾视之乃少年也 - Describes Xuanlü looking around and seeing a young man.
  • 某非常人即毗沙王之子那咤太子也 - The young man identifies himself as not an ordinary person but the son of King Vaisravana, Prince Nata.
  • 防法之故拥防和尚乆矣 - Indicates that due to the Dharma, the young man had been protecting the monk for a long time.
  • 贫道修行无事烦太子威神自在 - Xuanlü says he has no business that would trouble the prince's divine power.
  • 西域有可作佛事者愿太子致之 - Xuanlü asks the prince to bring something from the Western Regions that can be used for Buddhist activities.
  • 宣律求之即今崇圣寺佛牙是也 - Xuanlü requests the relic, which is now housed in the Chongsheng Temple.
  • 上令采蓝田緑玉琢为器上进簨簴流苏之属皆以金钿珠翠珍怪之物杂饰之 - Describes the emperor ordering the collection of green jade from Lantian to be carved into instruments, which were then adorned with gold, pearls, and other precious materials.
  • 又铸二金狮子作拏攫腾奋之状各重二百余斤以扶其他防绘缛丽制作神妙一时无比也 - Describes the casting of two gold lions in a fierce and dynamic posture, each weighing over 200 pounds, to support the instruments, which were beautifully decorated and crafted with divine skill.
  • 上幸蜀回京师乐器多亡失独玊磬偶在上顾之凄然不忍置于前促令送太常至今蔵于太常正乐库 - Describes the emperor's return to the capital after a visit to Shu, where many musical instruments were lost, but the jade chime was still there. The emperor, moved by its presence, ordered it to be sent to the Imperial Music Bureau, where it is still kept today.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 宣律 - A monk, likely a historical figure, who is the subject of the narrative.
  • 那咤太子 - Prince Nata, a figure from Indian mythology, known as the son of King Vaisravana.
  • 毗沙王 - King Vaisravana, one of the Four Heavenly Kings in Buddhist tradition.
  • 三蔵 - A reference to the Three Treasures (Buddha, Dharma, Sangha) in Buddhism.
  • 佛牙 - A Buddha's tooth relic, a significant object in Buddhist tradition.
  • 崇圣寺 - Chongsheng Temple, a historical temple in China.
  • 太真妃 - Empress Consort Taizhen, a historical figure known for her musical talents.
  • 蓝田緑玉 - Green jade from Lantian, a region known for its high-quality jade.
  • 上 - A reference to the emperor.

Proper Nouns

  • 宣律 - A monk.
  • 那咤太子 - Prince Nata.
  • 毗沙王 - King Vaisravana.
  • 三蔵 - The Three Treasures.
  • 佛牙 - Buddha's tooth relic.
  • 崇圣寺 - Chongsheng Temple.
  • 太真妃 - Empress Consort Taizhen.
  • 蓝田緑玉 - Green jade from Lantian.
  • 上 - The emperor.

[17]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 上所幸美人 - The emperor's favored concubine.
  • 忽梦人邀去纵酒 - Suddenly had a dream where someone invited her to drink freely.
  • 归辄流汗倦怠 - Upon returning, she would always sweat and feel tired.
  • 忽忽后因从容尽白于上 - Later, she calmly and completely told the emperor about it.
  • 此必术人所为也 - This must be the work of a magician or sorcerer.
  • 汝若复往但随宜以物识之 - If you go again, just mark it with something convenient.
  • 寤而具啓上 - She woke up and reported everything to the emperor.
  • 上乃潜以物色令于诸宫观求之 - The emperor then secretly sent someone to search all the palaces and temples.
  • 异日于东门观得其屏风手文尚在道士已遁矣 - One day, at the eastern gate temple, they found the screen with her handprint still on it, but the Taoist had already fled.
  • 口辩善奏对 - Eloquent and good at responding to imperial questions.
  • 上每嘉之 - The emperor always praised him.
  • 上令黄幡绰嘲之 - The emperor ordered Huang Fanchuo to mock him.
  • 文树切恶猿猴之号 - Liu Wenshu strongly disliked being called an ape.
  • 幡绰讯而进嘲曰 - Huang Fanchuo asked and then mocked him saying...
  • 猢孙强似文树 - Monkeys are stronger than Liu Wenshu.
  • 上知其赂遗大笑之 - The emperor knew about the bribe and laughed at it.
  • 平康坊南街废蛮院即李林甫旧宅也 - The abandoned foreigner's house on the south street of Pingkang Ward was once the old residence of Li Linfu.
  • 林甫于正堂后别创一堂制度弯曲有却月之形名曰月堂 - Li Linfu built a separate hall behind the main hall with a curved shape resembling a crescent moon, named it the Moon Hall.
  • 木土秀丽精巧当时莫俦也 - The wood and earth were beautiful and exquisite, unmatched at the time.
  • 及将败林甫于堂上见一物如人动遍体被毛毛如猪立锯牙钩爪三尺余以击林甫目如电光而怒视之 - When he was about to be defeated, Li Linfu saw something like a person move in the hall, covered in hair like a pig, with sharp teeth and claws over three feet long, striking his eyes like lightning and glaring angrily.
  • 林甫连叱不动遂命弧矢毛人笑而跳入前堂 - Li Linfu shouted repeatedly, but it didn't move, so he ordered arrows, and the hairy person laughed and jumped into the front hall.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 术人 - Magicians or sorcerers.
  • 安西衙将 - A military officer from the Anxi region, a historical region in Central Asia.
  • 黄幡绰 - A historical figure, a court jester known for his wit.
  • 李林甫 - A historical figure, a powerful chancellor during the Tang Dynasty known for his political machinations.
  • 平康坊 - A historical district in Chang'an (modern-day Xi'an), known for its entertainment and cultural activities.
  • 月堂 - A symbolic name for a hall, often associated with lunar deities or moon-related rituals.
  • 青衣 - Refers to a maid or servant, often dressed in blue or green.

Proper Nouns

  • 上 - The emperor.
  • 美人 - The emperor's favorite concubine.
  • 术人 - Magicians or sorcerers.
  • 黄幡绰 - Court jester Huang Fanchuo.
  • 李林甫 - Chancellor Li Linfu.
  • 平康坊 - A historical district in Chang'an.
  • 月堂 - The Moon Hall.
  • 青衣 - A maid or servant.

[18]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 常因妬媚有语侵上 - A compound phrase meaning 'often due to jealousy and flattery, she spoke in a way that offended the emperor.' Reflects a complex social dynamic where flattery can lead to unintended offense.
  • 上怒甚 - Means 'the emperor was very angry,' indicating a strong emotional reaction.
  • 妃悔恨号泣 - Means 'the concubine regretted and wept bitterly,' showing deep remorse.
  • 抽刀剪发授力士 - Means 'she drew a knife and cut her hair, presenting it to Gao Lishi,' a symbolic act of self-punishment.
  • 珠玉珍异皆上所赐不足充献 - Means 'jewels, precious stones, and rare items, all bestowed by the emperor, are insufficient for offering,' emphasizing the value of the gifts.
  • 惟发父母所生可达妾意 - Means 'only my hair, born of my parents, can express my feelings,' indicating the personal and emotional significance of the hair.
  • 望持此伸妾万一慕恋之诚 - Means 'I hope to hold this to express my slightest longing and attachment,' showing a plea for continued affection.
  • 上得髪挥涕悯然 - Means 'the emperor received the hair and shed tears, feeling compassion,' indicating a change in his emotional state.
  • 遽命力士召归 - Means 'he immediately ordered Gao Lishi to bring her back,' showing a swift decision.
  • 天寳初上遊华清宫 - Means 'in the early years of Tianbao, the emperor toured the Huaqing Palace,' setting the historical context.
  • 有刘朝霞者献贺幸温泉赋 - Means 'there was Liu Zhaoxia who presented a congratulatory poem for the emperor's visit to the hot springs,' introducing a literary figure.
  • 词调倜傥杂以俳谐 - Means 'the style is bold and varied, mixed with humor,' describing the poetic style.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 太真妃 - Refers to Yang Guifei, one of the most famous concubines of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 华清宫 - A famous imperial palace located in Xi'an, known for its hot springs and associated with the Tang dynasty.
  • 刘朝霞 - A poet who presented a congratulatory poem to the emperor during his visit to the hot springs.
  • 贺幸温泉赋 - A type of congratulatory poem, often used to celebrate the emperor's visits to significant places.
  • 珠玉珍异 - Refers to precious jewels and rare items, often used as gifts or offerings in court settings.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 常因妬媚有语侵上 - The phrase is elliptical and can be read as 'often due to jealousy and flattery, she spoke in a way that offended the emperor.'
  • 妃悔恨号泣抽刀剪发授力士 - The sequence of actions must be read as a series of events leading to the presentation of the hair.
  • 珠玉珍异皆上所赐不足充献 - The phrase is elliptical and can be read as 'jewels, precious stones, and rare items, all bestowed by the emperor, are insufficient for offering.'
  • 惟发父母所生可达妾意 - The phrase is elliptical and can be read as 'only my hair, born of my parents, can express my feelings.'
  • 望持此伸妾万一慕恋之诚 - The phrase is elliptical and can be read as 'I hope to hold this to express my slightest longing and attachment.'
  • 上得髪挥涕悯然 - The phrase is elliptical and can be read as 'the emperor received the hair and shed tears, feeling compassion.'
  • 遽命力士召归 - The phrase is elliptical and can be read as 'he immediately ordered Gao Lishi to bring her back.'
  • 天寳初上遊华清宫 - The phrase is elliptical and can be read as 'in the early years of Tianbao, the emperor toured the Huaqing Palace.'
  • 有刘朝霞者献贺幸温泉赋 - The phrase is elliptical and can be read as 'there was Liu Zhaoxia who presented a congratulatory poem for the emperor's visit to the hot springs.'
  • 词调倜傥杂以俳谐 - The phrase is elliptical and can be read as 'the style is bold and varied, mixed with humor.'

Proper Nouns

  • 太真妃 - Yang Guifei, a famous concubine of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 华清宫 - A famous imperial palace located in Xi'an, known for its hot springs and associated with the Tang dynasty.
  • 刘朝霞 - A poet who presented a congratulatory poem to the emperor during his visit to the hot springs.
  • 贺幸温泉赋 - A type of congratulatory poem, often used to celebrate the emperor's visits to significant places.
  • 珠玉珍异 - Refers to precious jewels and rare items, often used as gifts or offerings in court settings.
